front: Novel: opens with June Kashpaw freezing to death while walking home in a snowstorm to a Chippewa reservation
Novel: Lipsha plans to use goose hearts (since geese mate for life) to make the title substance and cement the relationship between Marie and Nector Kashpaw
Novel: features the love triangle of Marie Lazarre, Lulu Lamartine, and Nector Kashpaw on a North Dakota Ojibwe reservation
Short Story: after returning from Vietnam, Henry jumps into a river and tells his brother Lyman 'my boots are filling' before drowning; Lyman drives the title vehicle into the river
Novel: Joe Coutts investigates the assault of his mother Geraldine by Linden Lark; part of a 'Justice Trilogy' about sexual violence on reservations
Novel: 2020 Pulitzer winner inspired by the author's grandfather's resistance to Native termination policies at Turtle Mountain
Novel: an unfaithful wife sacrifices her daughter to wolves while traveling over a frozen lake; the bones are placed in the title object
Novel: in 'Saint Marie,' a Satan-obsessed nun pours scalding water into the title character's ear
Author: co-wrote The Crown of Columbus with husband Michael Dorris; studied creative writing under John Barth
